reviews.review_description: I want to like this game but I don’t. Got stuck/ lost in the tutorial( not sure I finished it). In the game one misstep has you rock climbing your way out of pits. That’d be ok if it mattered. Guns sound like pellet guns. I must admit other sounds in the game were nice. The Metallica reference was cool. You think this is fun? You think this is awful? You’re right their other game. Check out their other game Nock!

I like the concept of the game and the movement mechanics. The level design is okay. The game is fun. What I don't like about it is that you could easily lose your gun in the game, which is annoying. You can drop it at any moment, and for that I have to drop a star.

Joking aside please have the weapons auto holster. The game is about speed and maneuvers. It should feel dynamic and smooth. Losing the gun because of a misclick is just annoying.

Occasionally I'll purchase a game solely based on the art direction. This is one of those games.

It looked interesting on YouTube but when I got in there myself I was really blown away.

It's a surrealistic masterpiece!

The landscape, color palette, enemy design, animation, sound effects, and music are all so well done.

The artists, sound designers, and musicians on this team are brilliant!

I haven't played much of it yet but so far it's been exhilarating to play. You can launch yourself up ridiculously high by pressing "A" while flinging your arm down, then as you float back down you can turn around and steer in mid air. Targeting enemies in free fall this way is incredibly fun.

I've only tried two weapons so far and they both feel solid. Tracer rounds make targeting fast and easy. 

The reload mechanic is unusual. You press "B" and the spent clip ejects and a new clip materializes in the air. Then you smack the weapon down on the floating clip.  It's hard to explain in words but it's oddly satisfying.

reviews.review_title: Jank is not an aesthetic. Make choices.
reviews.review_description:
This is a good simple concept with decent presentation and solid gameplay. They just need to tighten up some of the graphics in level 3.
Guns being misaligned in a standalone game seems careless. It's ok for things to be low-poly or lofi but things should feel unsettingly deliberate not lazy.

After playing few games. You definitely need a team that's number one. And I love the concept how it's a capture the flag but with a campaign story mixed with inception and horde mode style. 

Dear developers, 

The controllers could be a little better and please make sure it's also compatible to PCVR for steam. I think with another few fixes on the game to make it solid. It would be perfect like the game Synapse PS VR2. For those who play you know exactly what I'm talking about.

reviews.review_title: Co-op is more fun than single player
reviews.review_description:
Rather enjoyed the couple hours on the game so far. Love the old school Halo vibe. 

I bounced off the tutorial the first time, but jumped back in with some friends and liked it much more. Really is faster paced compared to most VR games, and I like it. 

Worthwhile buy in my opinion.

The art style is solid, and the whole aesthetic makes the world feel super fun to be in. The locomotion is spot on, and the gunplay feels satisfying.
The level design is really fun imo and the game isn't to hand holdy

such fast and fluid momentum in such a big stylized world made for fun co-op or solo thumping shooting runs! gunplay is also wonderful, frantic and addictive, but it's really the momentum that keeps you going (pun intended).

there's room for improvement as a few bugs come in the way, but mostly minor glitches

Not worth more than $10 at the moment. Short 5-7h linear campaign offering little replay value. Decent gameplay, but could be faster. The game feels unfinished, only five enemy types and missing many basic quality-of-life features. There’s a clunky, unintuitive way to reload faster while dual-wielding (I only figured it out from YouTube, and it's a must). Coop can be frustrating since revives are shared. Love the music and art. Overall disappointing.

There is no direction. Im guessing everyone else who bought this game is stuck on level 3? Wtf do you do? There's no objective. I really like the style too and mechanics.  But make a better more informative tutorial and it's a 5 stars game!
As soon as I opened it BAM triggering hands and when I grab a gun I was holding it crucid
